title: useImperativeHandle
body:
## Definition

**`useImperativeHandle`** customizes the instance value exposed when a parent calls `ref` on a child wrapped in `forwardRef`.

## Why it exists

Expose imperative methods (focus, scroll, play) on reusable components without leaking full DOM nodes.

## How it works

```tsx
useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
  focus: () => inputRef.current?.focus(),
}), []);
```

Parent: `ref.current?.focus()`.

## Common confusion

Prefer declarative props when possible — imperative handles are escape hatches. Must pair with `forwardRef`.

## In practice

Input wrappers, media players, and design-system components integrating non-React libraries.

## Definition

**[useImperativeHandle](concept:use-imperative-handle)** customizes the instance value exposed when a parent [calls](concept:call-apply-bind) `ref` on a child wrapped in [forwardRef](concept:forward-ref).

## Why it exists

Expose imperative methods (focus, scroll, play) on reusable components without leaking full [DOM](concept:dom) nodes.

## How it works

```tsx
useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
  focus: () => inputRef.current?.focus(),
}), []);
```

Parent: `ref.current?.focus()`.

## Common confusion

Prefer declarative props when possible — imperative handles are escape hatches. Must pair with [forwardRef](concept:forward-ref).

## In practice

Input wrappers, media players, and design-system components integrating non-React libraries.
